prompt: a quiet working-day moment in a stone atelier - the attached product(s) sit naturally beside a stone sample, tool, or cloth on a polished workbench; let the framing feel observed, not staged. Wide 16:9 hero, product/action right-of-center, left third open as continuous stone/workshop context. Documentary product photo, Romanian stone atelier or stone setting, natural daylight, true material texture. Must read as a real photograph, not a CGI/AI render: coherent directional light, true contact shadows, shallow depth of field with lens softness, uneven reflections, fine micro-texture - premium but never artificially perfect. Stone keeps natural veining and saw marks; packaging shows real plastic, curved labels and edge shadows; any wet or polished surface keeps uneven streaks and real specular highlights, never mirror gloss. ARDmag: packaging follows references; workwear says "ARC-ROM Diamonds", never ARDmag on clothes. Avoid: CGI/3D-render or AI-plastic look, waxy skin, impossible mirror reflections, over-clean symmetry, glossy mockup polish, flat uniform studio lighting. Avoid fake labels, packshots, white halo, floating products, SaaS wallpaper, clipping. one continuous photographic scene - no montage, no collage, no split panels, no pasted cutouts or scissor edges; no fine text in image; any in-image text: no Romanian diacritics; packaging faithful to reference - full label with all its text rows and badges, never simplified; reference label text is the only permitted fine text; integrated naturally, never pasted.
